A.name: The CTA Leak
A.desc: Plain-English booking-process angle. Leads with the $5K-$15K/week leak. Three issues, tease the third. Close with calendar or sample audit.

beats.title: Booking Process Leak
beats.line: Hi, my name is {caller} with Conversion Exotics — I review exotic and luxury car rental sites for a living. I pulled up your site, and your booking process is costing you roughly $5,000 to $15,000 a week in booked rentals. Do you know who I can talk to about this?
responses: What do you mean costing me?
beats.followup: Got it — quick context on what I'm seeing, then I'll show you exactly where the money's going.
beats.note: Calling the business, not a person. No name tokens. No apology hedges.

beats.title: 3-Second Decision
beats.line: Quick context — when a luxury renter taps your ad from their phone, they decide in about 3 seconds whether you're worth waiting on. Every one-second delay cuts conversions roughly 32 percent. Most exotic rental sites load in 5 to 8 seconds on mobile. When was the last time someone on your team pulled your site up on a phone and booked a rental end-to-end?
beats.followup: Yeah — that's what most owners say. The site loading is just the first leak. The bigger ones come after.
beats.note: Plain English. Mobile speed as door-opener, not the main point.

beats.title: The Three Leaks
beats.line: Here's what's actually happening. A renter taps your ad, counts to three, and opens the next one — they never even see your fleet. The ones who wait it out land on a page that doesn't show a price, doesn't show the car they came for, and asks them to fill out a form before they've decided anything. By the time they figure out how to book, they've already opened two competitors in other tabs. That's the $5,000 to $15,000 a week. Cleaning up the top of the page and making the booking obvious — those are week-one quick wins. But there's a third issue on your booking process that costs more than both combined. I'd rather show you that one with your page open.
responses: How much would this cost to fix?
beats.followup: Fair — and that's exactly what the audit answers. Pricing comes after.
beats.note: Tease the third issue. Do NOT reveal it on the call. The reveal is the audit.

beats.title: 100+ Sites, Same 3 Leaks
beats.line: I've done this on over 100 exotic and luxury car rental sites — same 10-point checklist, same booking process, same three leaks every time. The shops that fix the top two see bookings climb in the first week. The ones that fix all three usually double their weekend bookings inside 30 days.
responses: How long does the fix take?
beats.followup: Fair — and that's exactly why I lead with the audit, not the pitch.
beats.note: Credibility frame. 100+ sites. Same checklist. Pattern recognition.

beats.title: Free $3,000 Audit
beats.line: Here's what I want to do. I run a $3,000 conversion audit — and I'd like to do yours free. Thirty minutes. Your live site on screen. Every leak named in plain English no agency would dare use, with dollar-anchored estimates per leak. You walk away with a sequenced one-week plan — every fix shipped at once, ranked by booking impact. Yours to keep whether you hire me to ship it or not. Not vibes — math.
beats.followup: No catch — I'm running a handful of these personally this quarter for case studies. That's the only ask.
beats.note: Verbatim site language. $3K free. One-week plan. All fixes shipped at once.

beats.title: Calendar or Sample
beats.line: Two ways we can do this. I've got Thursday at 10 or Friday at 2 open — pick one and I'll send the calendar invite. Or, if you'd rather see what this looks like first, give me your email and I'll send over a sample audit I did for another exotic rental shop — full plan, dollar numbers, the whole thing. Which one would you prefer?
beats.followup: Done — calendar invite or sample audit hits your inbox in 60 seconds.
beats.note: Dual-path close. Booked call OR sample audit. Either is a win.

B.name: Next Ad Steals It
B.desc: Side-by-side competitor steal angle. Leads with the 3-second tap-back. Same $5K-$15K/week anchor. Flip-the-steal audit framing.

beats.title: Next Listing Steal
beats.line: Hi, my name is {caller} with Conversion Exotics — I review exotic and luxury car rental sites for a living. Quick question — do you know how many renters tap your ad, count to three, and book the next listing instead? Because on your site right now, that's costing you $5,000 to $15,000 a week. Who's the right person to talk to about this?
responses: What do you mean costing me?
beats.followup: Got it — quick context on what I'm seeing, then I'll show you exactly where the money's going.
beats.note: Calling the business, not a person. Question-first hook lands the steal frame immediately.

beats.title: Side-By-Side Speed Gap
beats.line: Quick context — I pulled your site up next to two other exotic shops in your market on a phone. They loaded in 2 to 3 seconds. Yours took closer to 6. That's the gap. A luxury renter decides in 3 seconds whether to wait — every one-second delay drops conversions roughly 32 percent. When was the last time someone on your team pulled your site up on a phone, side-by-side with a competitor's?
beats.followup: Yeah — that's what most owners say. The site loading is just the first leak. The bigger ones come after.
beats.note: Side-by-side reframe makes the speed gap concrete. The 32% stat does the agitation lift.

beats.title: Competitor Gets The Weekend
beats.line: Here's what's actually happening. The renter taps your ad, counts to three, and the next shop's site is already loading. They tap it. They book. Your competitor gets the $3,500 weekend — you never even showed up on their screen. The ones who do wait land on a page that doesn't show a price, doesn't show the car they came for, and asks them to fill out a form before they've decided anything. So they open two more tabs and forget you. That's the $5,000 to $15,000 a week. Fixing the load and making the booking obvious — those are week-one quick wins. But there's a third issue on your booking process that costs more than both combined. I'd rather show you that one with your page open.
responses: How much would this cost to fix?
beats.followup: Fair — and that's exactly what the audit answers. Pricing comes after.
beats.note: Tease the third issue. Do NOT reveal it on the call. The reveal is the audit.

beats.title: 100+ Sites Audited
beats.line: I've audited over 100 exotic and luxury car rental sites — same 10-point checklist, same booking process, same three leaks every time. The shops that fix the top two see bookings climb in the first week. The ones that fix all three usually double their weekend bookings inside 30 days.
responses: How long does the fix take?
beats.followup: Fair — and that's exactly why I lead with the audit, not the pitch.
beats.note: Credibility frame. 100+ sites. Same checklist. Pattern recognition.

beats.title: Flip The Steal — Free $3,000 Audit
beats.line: Here's what I want to do. I run a $3,000 conversion audit — and I'd like to do yours free. Thirty minutes. Your live site on screen. I show you every leak, attach a dollar number to each one, and hand you a one-week plan to flip the steal — so the next time both your ads load side-by-side, yours is the one closing the booking. Yours to keep whether you hire me to ship it or not. Not vibes — math.
beats.followup: No catch — I'm running a handful of these personally this quarter for case studies. That's the only ask.
beats.note: Steal-frame hook ties audit back to Beat 3 imagery. One-week plan, all fixes shipped at once.

beats.title: Calendar or Sample
beats.line: Two ways we can do this. I've got Thursday at 10 or Friday at 2 open — pick one and I'll send the calendar invite, and we'll get your site flipped from the one losing the click to the one closing it. Or, if you'd rather see what this looks like first, give me your email and I'll send over a sample audit I did for another exotic rental shop.
beats.followup: Done — calendar invite or sample audit hits your inbox in 60 seconds.
beats.note: Dual-path close. Steal-frame tilt on calendar path. Either choice is a win.

C.name: 3-Issue Reveal
C.desc: Names two of three issues outright in plain English (top of page + form), teases the third for the audit. $5K-$15K/week anchor. Dual-path close.

beats.title: Three Issues Frame
beats.line: Hi, my name is {caller} with Conversion Exotics — I review exotic and luxury car rental sites for a living. I pulled up your site and I'm seeing three specific issues on your booking process that together are costing you roughly $5,000 to $15,000 a week in booked rentals. Do you know who I can talk to about this?
beats.followup: Got it — quick run-through of what I'm seeing, then I'll show you exactly where the money's going.
beats.note: Calling the business, not a person. Three-issues hook sets up the reveal.

beats.title: Three Stuck Spots
beats.line: Here's the deal — every exotic rental site I look at has three spots where renters get stuck between tapping the ad and actually booking the car: the top of the page, the part where they fill out their info, and one more spot most owners never notice. On your site, all three are losing you money. For every extra second your site takes to load, you lose about a third of your bookings — and that's just the first one.
beats.followup: Yeah — that's the most common reaction. The reveal is in the audit.
beats.note: Plain-English version of CRO failure stack. No jargon. Tee up the reveal in Beat 3.

beats.title: Walk Through Two Of Three
beats.line: So here's what's actually happening. The top of your page doesn't show a price and doesn't show the car they came for — so they back out and tap the next shop. The ones who scroll down hit your booking form — you're asking them to fill out their info before they've even decided to book. That's a wall. They open two more tabs and forget you. That's the $5,000 to $15,000 a week. Those two are week-one quick wins. But there's a third spot on your booking process that costs more than both of those combined. I'd rather show you that one with your page open.
responses: How much would this cost to fix?
beats.followup: Fair — and that's exactly what the audit answers. Pricing comes after.
beats.note: Reveal two issues plain. Tease third. Do NOT name the third on the call — the reveal is the audit.

beats.line: I've audited over 100 exotic and luxury car rental sites — same 10-point checklist, same booking process, same three leaks every time. The shops that fix the top two see bookings climb in the first week. The ones that fix all three usually double their weekend bookings inside 30 days.
responses: How long does the fix take?
beats.followup: Fair — and that's exactly why I lead with the audit, not the pitch.
beats.note: Credibility frame. 100+ sites. Same checklist. Pattern recognition.
